meta.name: Global X HSCEI Covered Call Active ETF
meta.issuer: Global X (Mirae Asset Global Investments HK)
meta.fund_manager: Mirae Asset Global Investments (Hong Kong) Limited
meta.strategy: Active — physical HSCEI basket + covered-call overlay
meta.score_methodology: Absolute (peer-free) ETF market-quality score (0–100): weighted blend of time-weighted spread, median ADV turnover, quote uptime, displayed depth at L1 and inside the ±5% mid-price band (no cross-ETF peer universe).

short_block.trend_label: Rising — last 7 days +66% vs prior week

profile_cards.title: Dominant segment
profile_cards.label: Institutional-sized stable
profile_cards.text: Dominant by turnover after classifying continuous trades with internal same-direction execution groups.

profile_cards.title: Institutional-sized stable
profile_cards.label: Low observed impact
profile_cards.text: Large executions with limited price movement, limited post-run quote impact, and depth usage within displayed liquidity.

profile_cards.title: Institutional-sized active
profile_cards.label: Large non-stable executions
profile_cards.text: Large executions that are not stable. These may include higher depth usage, price movement, or stronger quote interaction, but no aggressor label is assigned.

profile_cards.title: Short-volume overlay
profile_cards.label: Elevated market-maker throughput
profile_cards.text: Short volume is high for the latest 30-day window. For an ETF this often reflects market-maker inventory and creation/redemption facilitation, not directional bearish flow. L3 shows 1,887,387 continuous-session order-book messages over the same 30-day segment window, with 5.6% refreshing the best quote.

profile_cards.title: Classification confidence
profile_cards.label: Evidence-weighted
profile_cards.text: Average internal execution-group confidence. Lower scores mean the observable tape supports a broad behavior, not a precise actor label.

method.name: ETF execution segment classification
method.unit_of_classification: internal same-direction execution groups; display uses trades and turnover, not people or accounts
method.universe: continuous-session printable board-lot trades only; auction trades are excluded from execution segment shares
method.direction_inference: Continuous trades are matched to the latest prior valid L2 best bid/ask within one second and classified as buy/sell/unknown by quote location.
method.run_grouping: A new run starts when inferred direction changes, the time gap is greater than one millisecond, session changes, direction is unknown, or a same-direction safety price jump exceeds max(10 ticks, 50 bps).
method.critical_guardrail: Institutional-sized active is not an identity or aggressor label; it means the large execution does not meet the strict stable-execution test.
method.l3_usage: L3 data is used only for aggregate quote-maintenance diagnostics because this feed does not expose account, broker, or market-maker identity.
method.short_usage: The short-volume overlay is the 30-day average short-sale turnover share from the exchange short-sale tape.

insights.overall: After classifying continuous-session trades into execution segments, 3416 is Institutional-sized stable by 30-day turnover (36.1%). Mixed tickets are 32.0% of trades, while institutional-sized segments are 47.7% of value.
insights.retail: Retail-sized executions are 43.2% of trades but only 12.5% of turnover, so small executions are visible without controlling the value traded.
insights.mixed: Mixed tickets are 32.0% of trades and 31.9% of turnover, separating mid-sized activity from true institutional-sized executions.
insights.institutional: Institutional-sized stable and active segments account for 47.7% of turnover (36.1% stable, 11.5% active).
insights.quality_check: The model does not label parent orders directly. Institutional-sized active is the residual large-execution segment after the strict stable-execution test.
insights.directionality: Continuous-session turnover is 59.4% buy-initiated, 32.6% sell-initiated, and 8.0% unclassified by L2 quote location.
insights.etf_specific: Short volume is high for the latest 30-day window. For an ETF this often reflects market-maker inventory and creation/redemption facilitation, not directional bearish flow. L3 shows 1,887,387 continuous-session order-book messages over the same 30-day segment window, with 5.6% refreshing the best quote.
insights.methodology: Execution segment shares use only continuous-session printable board-lot trades, so the visible mix sums to 100% without auction flow. Retail-sized means below max(HK$100K, 2 board lots by reference mid); institutional-sized starts at max(HK$1M, 20 board lots by reference mid), with mixed tickets between those thresholds. Each trade is matched to the latest prior valid L2 quote within one second. Classification is built from internal same-direction execution groups: a new group starts when direction changes, session changes, the gap exceeds one millisecond, direction is unknown, or the safety price-jump rule is triggered. Price movement is measured inside that group using dynamic ETF tick size. The report displays trades and turnover, not internal group counts. No account, broker, or market-maker identity is inferred.

current.label: Deep / tight liquidity
current.description: High displayed depth, tight spreads, low realized volatility, and enough activity to validate the quotes.
current.issuer_read: Secondary-market liquidity is functioning well. The issuer should watch whether large active flow starts widening spreads or reducing displayed depth.

drivers.label: Spread regime
drivers.text: Latest spread sits in the extreme tight-spread zone of the model sample.

drivers.label: Displayed L1 depth
drivers.text: Top-of-book liquidity is high versus the sample, which supports the deep-liquidity label.

drivers.label: Turnover intensity
drivers.text: Activity is above normal; this confirms that the tight spread is being observed on a real trading day, not an inactive quote.

drivers.label: Realized volatility
drivers.text: Low 10-day realized volatility argues against a current stress/repricing regime.

drivers.label: Trade-trigger skew
drivers.text: Negative means sell-triggered flow is larger. Today is sell-leaning, but not large enough to override the tight-spread/depth signal.

drivers.label: Short-volume overlay
drivers.text: For an ETF this is treated as market-maker throughput evidence unless it also coincides with wider spreads, falling depth, or NAV dislocation.

drivers.label: Execution segment mix
drivers.text: Institutional-sized segments dominate 1M turnover: stable 36.1%, active 11.5%.

drivers.label: L3 quote maintenance
drivers.text: 1,887,387 continuous-session L3 messages in the 30D execution segment window; best-quote refresh is a maintenance/market-making signal, not identity.

regimes.label: Deep / tight liquidity
regimes.description: High displayed depth, tight spreads, low realized volatility, and enough activity to validate the quotes.
regimes.issuer_read: Secondary-market liquidity is functioning well. The issuer should watch whether large active flow starts widening spreads or reducing displayed depth.

regimes.label: High-activity liquidity taking
regimes.description: High turnover with more aggressive trade-trigger imbalance or active demand, but not necessarily a disorderly market.
regimes.issuer_read: This is the regime where execution quality matters most: demand is active, so monitor spread resilience, price walk, and market-maker replenishment.

regimes.label: Thin / wide liquidity
regimes.description: Lower turnover and displayed depth with wider spreads; poor screen liquidity can matter even without large price moves.
regimes.issuer_read: This is a market-making coverage regime. Focus on quote depth, spread obligations, and whether enough liquidity providers are active.

regimes.label: Stress / repricing
regimes.description: High volatility or large price moves, often with elevated impact risk. This is the regime where liquidity quality can deteriorate fast.
regimes.issuer_read: Treat this as an escalation regime: check premium/discount, underlying-market stress, creation/redemption capacity, and market-maker quoting continuity.

insights.title: Market-quality read
insights.text: Current spread 5.2 bps, L1 depth HK$69.9M, and 10D vol 2.9% put 3416 in deep / tight liquidity.

insights.title: Flow read
insights.text: Trade-trigger flow is 45.5% buy vs 54.5% sell. Because spread and depth are strong, this reads as absorbed flow, not stress.

insights.title: ETF plumbing read
insights.text: High short-volume share is important, but for ETFs it is a market-maker inventory and creation-redemption proxy unless confirmed by spread, depth, or NAV stress.

insights.title: Segment read
insights.text: Institutional-sized execution segments dominate turnover, so issuer monitoring should focus on execution quality and replenishment, not only trade count.

method.name: Prototype-anchored daily regime clustering
method.why_this_method: A full Markov-switching/HMM model is statistically attractive, but with roughly one year of daily ETF data it can overfit. This report uses a transparent prototype-anchored clustering layer, then explains the label with observable microstructure factors.
guardrails: Regimes are behavioral market states, not causal proof.
Short-volume is an ETF liquidity-provision overlay, not a standalone bearish sentiment indicator.
L3 data is used for aggregate quote-maintenance behavior only; it does not identify market makers, brokers, or accounts.
News/event tags should be used as annotations around detected breaks, not forced into the model without a clean event dataset.

narratives.exec_subtitle: Global X HSCEI Covered Call Active ETF (03416 · HKEX): 8 designated MMs · AUM HK$22.4B · TER 0.75%.
key_takeaways: 3416 earns 93.9/100 on our absolute ETF market-quality score (Excellent tier), with 5.5 bps spread and 97.5% quote uptime — not benchmarked vs other ETFs.
30-day turnover HK$239.9M/day; displayed aggregate depth inside the ±5% mid-price band averages around HK$301.2M per side — shown as price-band depth, not a guaranteed executable allotment.
Short volume ran 45% of turnover over the last 30 days — read as MM hedging throughput, not investor sentiment for this product type.
narratives.liquidity_overall: 3416 trades cleanly for an HK-listed ETF in its category. Over the last 30 days, the time-weighted spread averaged 5.5 bps, with both sides of the book quoted 97.5% of the continuous session. Average daily turnover of HK$239.9M (2156 trades/day) is supported by 8 designated market makers, the strongest signal of a healthy MM coverage in this product.
narratives.spread_trend: The trajectory is favourable: spreads tightened by 45% versus the trailing year average (10.0 bps → 5.5 bps), reflecting maturing MM coverage as AUM grew to HK$22.4B.
narratives.depth_text: Top-of-book depth has grown 30% versus the trailing year — typical of a maturing MM ecosystem.
narratives.composition_text: After classifying continuous-session trades into execution segments, 3416 is Institutional-sized stable by 30-day turnover (36.1%). Mixed tickets are 32.0% of trades, while institutional-sized segments are 47.7% of value.
narratives.intraday_text: Intraday volume is moderately concentrated (HHI 0.142). The continuous session captures 99.2% of turnover; 0.1% trades in the closing auction and 0.7% in the opening auction.
narratives.short_text: Short volume averages 44.8% of total turnover over the last 30 days (peak 84.0%). For an ETF this is largely structural — market makers short while creating units to facilitate primary-market activity, so this should be read as MM throughput, not directional sentiment. Correlation with return is mildly negative (-0.197); episodes of high short% loosely coincide with down days, but the link is weak.

regime_performance.covered_call_interpretation: Favorable: option premium is relatively rich while equity direction is not dominated by a sharp rally.
Mixed: liquidity and funding shifts coincide with unstable volatility and equity direction.
Mixed to unfavorable versus HSCEI if the equity rally is sharp, because the overwrite can cap upside.
Unfavorable: option income cushions but does not eliminate equity beta in broad drawdowns.
